ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-06-2021 Continued To Jan-19-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kelly McGuire, via ZOOM, and Vincent Yu, via ZOOM, for Arthur Vigil, Administrator.

The court notes that there is an issue with the incomplete petition caption and no mention of a lien. The court also notes the incorrect math in the statutory attorney's fee calculation and queries counsel if they would like a continuance to address the preceding. Mr. Yu presents oral advocacy in support of the petition. The court also advises that there are several aspects of the requests for advanced costs and that additional information is required for clarification.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is continued to Jan-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution

Off Calendar Aug-25-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-28-2021 Continued To Aug-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall, via ZOOM, for Arthur Vigil, Personal Representative.

The court begins by stating that this matter has been previously continued four times. Counsel reports that the petition has been signed and submitted and is currently in the e-filing queue.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to August 25, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-30-2021 Continued To Jul-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall appeared by zoom for Arthur Vigil (Administrator).

The Court noted that at the last hearing of May 19th Ms. Hall was very confident that she would have the petition filed in 30-days and questions why a petition has not been filed. Ms. Hall stated that the petition is done, that she has to do a final review and get it out to everybody this week and that it's a final petition on waiver of account. Status on close of estate is continued to July 28, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-19-2021 Continued To Jun-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall appeared by zoom for Arthur Vigil (Administrator).
Sheila Robello (Former Counsel for Arthur Vigil) appeared by zoom.

Ms. Hall requested a 30-day continuance stating that she just received everybody's final expenses and costs, that now that she has the information, she can do a petition and get it on file, and that there is an agreement to share the petition with Mr. Boessenecker. Status on close of estate is continued to June 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hall is to have her petition on file by June 16, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Between Arthur Vigil, David Middleton, Daniel Middleton, And Gina Henson
Off Calendar Feb-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Approval Of Creditors Claim
Off Calendar Feb-16-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall appeared by zoom for Arthur Vigil (Administrator).
Vincent Yu (Litigation Cousel) appeared by zoom for Arthur Vigil.
John Boessenecker appeared by zoom for Gina Henderson.

Mr. Yu stated that if the settlement agreement is approved, the other two petitions can go off calendar. It was noted that the request for this Court to reserve jurisdiction pursuant to CCP 664.6 to enforce the terms of the settlement agreement is in paragraph 18 of the petition. The Court noted that all Parties have signed the agreement. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement Between Arthur Vigil, David Middleton, Daniel Middleton, and Gina Henson (order signed in open Court). The Petition for Approval of Creditor's Claim and the Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights are off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights

Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for Arthur Vigil.
Randall Boessenecker for Gina Henson (Objector).

Ms. Robello stated that she just received discovery which is due November 8th, that they have discussed spending a short amount of time on doing discovery and the possibility of mediation, that they need 60-days to do discovery, and that they will then come back and perhaps request a referral to mediation. The Court questions why the parties don't want to be referred to mediation now. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 14, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 21, 2020. The Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights and the Petition for Approval of Creditor's Claim are continued to and the Parties are to return on March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Creditors Claim
Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Inheritance Rights
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Continued To Oct-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Button for Arthur Vigil (Petitioner).
Randall Boesenecker for Gina Henson (Objector).

Mr. Boesenecker requested a 60-day continuance stating that he has some discovery out going, and that he is expecting a response to that and then they will work on settlement. Ms. Button stated that the talks are friendly. The Court noted that it has an issue with potential conflict of the Administrator also being a Creditor and stated that Counsel might want to think about how best to manage the conflict. Ms. Button stated that they have consulted with Attorney Mercy Hall to handle the estate. The Petition to Determine Inheritance Rights and the Petition for Approval of Creditor's Claim are continued to October 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Arthur Vigil.

Mr. Lachona stated that there is no objection to the petition, that both of the Brothers have retained Counsel, that he has a tentative agreement with one of the Brothers, that the other Brother is speaking with his Attorney about the agreement and that there is no objection to having his Client appointed. The Court noted that the Petitioner has filed a claim to 100% of the Estate, questions whether the Petitioner can remain impartial and raised the issue of the Administrator not using Estate assets in what would be his personal fight. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order) stating that Mr. Vigil has been nominated by one of the nominal heirs and there is no objection. Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for January 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2018 Continued To Jul-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Petitioner

Mr. Lachona stated that he found no legal authority requiring creditors to have separate counsel, that protection is already in place, he would either get consent from beneficiaries or seek court's review and approval of creditor's claim in the form of a noticed petition, and he has talked with the beneficiaries' attorneys about the terms of administering the estate. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to July 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Continued To Jul-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
